description: Autodesk is a leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, providing software and service solutions that address the entire value chain of engineering, architecture, construction, product design, and digital content creation.
vision_statement: To empower creativity, anywhere - on any device - and to help our customers solve some of the world's most complex and pressing challenges.
mission_statement: To help people imagine, design and create a better world.
sales_motion: Autodesk's sales motion typically involves a consultative approach, where sales representatives work closely with customers to understand their specific needs and provide tailored solutions.
exit_strategy_history: Autodesk has a history of successful acquisitions, including the purchase of Alias, Softimage, and Sketchbook.
carbon_footprint: Autodesk has set a goal to be carbon neutral by 2025 and has made significant reductions in its greenhouse gas emissions in recent years.
ethical_sourcing: Autodesk has a strong commitment to ethical sourcing and has implemented various policies and programs to ensure responsible sourcing practices.
benchmark_vs_peers: Autodesk's financial performance is generally in line with its peers, with a market capitalization of around $30 billion.
case_studies: Autodesk Helps City of Los Angeles Reduce Carbon Footprint
sustainability_csr: Autodesk has a strong commitment to sustainability and has implemented various initiatives to reduce its environmental impact.
crisis_behavior: Autodesk has a well-established crisis management plan in place, which includes regular communication with stakeholders and a focus on transparency and accountability.
